suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales
volcanic glass and its use in prehistoric surgery
extra_system_rules: Sound like a real person on Reddit or Twitter, NOT like a philosophy textbook.
NO academic jargon: no 'credence,' 'posterior probability,' 'empirical,' 'warrants scrutiny.'
Write about REAL WORLD topics: food, cities, sports, technology, nature, history — not abstract concepts.

formats.name: micro_mythbuster
formats.instruction: Choose a tiny myth or common misconception and debunk it in under 30 words. Open with 'Myth:' and end with the real fact.

formats.name: future_fragment
formats.instruction: Write a brief scene set 10 years from now, focusing on one detail that has changed. No exposition—just describe what the reader sees or hears. 50-75 words.

formats.name: question_chain
formats.instruction: Ask a series of 4-7 thought-provoking questions about a single topic, each building on the one before. No statements allowed—only questions.

formats.name: personal_parallel
formats.instruction: Draw an unexpected parallel between your life and a random concept (e.g., ‘My job is like a vending machine because…’). Start with the parallel, then explain in 100-180 words.

formats.name: contrarian_short
formats.instruction: Deliver a 1-2 sentence take that goes against conventional wisdom. Open with 'Everyone thinks...', then immediately present your flip-side. 25-40 words.

formats.name: threaded_memory
formats.instruction: Narrate a chain of 3 related memories or flashes, each just 1-2 lines, linked by a repeating word or motif. Use poetic language. Total: 50-90 words.

formats.name: utopian_blueprint
formats.instruction: Sketch a brief blueprint describing how you would redesign an annoying system or process (e.g., commutes, meetings, laundry) from scratch. Use 3 sections: 'The Problem,' 'The Dream,' 'Why It Could Work.' 200-300 words.

formats.name: visualization_prompt
formats.instruction: Guide the reader through a vivid mental image related to a topic, using only sensory details. Do NOT explain—just describe. 40-70 words.

formats.name: one_pager
formats.instruction: Condense what people need to know about a complex subject into a single, dense, but readable paragraph—no list, no headers, just pure insight. 300-350 words.

title_styles: Write a title framing the post as a confession or secret revealed.
Write a title that starts with 'How I realized...' or 'What nobody told me about...'
Write a title as a provocative question that challenges assumptions.
Write a playful title that riffs on a pop culture reference.
Write a title using alliteration and rhythm for punchiness.
structure_variants: Break the post into numbered fragments or vignettes—each must stand alone but build on each other.
Begin and end with the exact same sentence for a circular effect.
Integrate a rhetorical question at every section break.
Scatter single-word sentences or fragments throughout for impact.
End with a visual metaphor or analogy.
topic_angles: How everyday technology shapes our relationships.
What your childhood hobbies reveal about your adult choices.
The secret rituals we create in boring routines.
Why people defend things they know are flawed.
Unexpected side effects of life hacks.
